message:
Add comprehensive e2e/harness test coverage improvements

- Add tests for randomID with various lengths and edge cases
- Add tests for containsAny with boundary conditions
- Add tests for classifyCommand covering all risk tiers
- Add tests for testConfig verifying timeout settings
- Add tests for WriteTestFile with nested directories
- Add tests for MustPath path construction
- Add tests for multiple sessions and requests workflow
- Add tests for DBState logging
- Add tests for LogBuffer operations
- Add tests for Step and Result logging methods

Several internal functions now have 100% coverage:
- testConfig, classifyCommand, containsAny, MustPath
- GetRequestStatus, ApproveRequest, RejectRequest

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"simultaneous_launch_button-d19.2","title":"Implement Agent Mail integration","description":"## Task
Implement Agent Mail integration for cross-agent notifications.

## When Request Created
Send notification to SLB-Reviews thread with:
- Command (truncated/redacted)
- Risk tier
- Requestor info
- Justification summary
- Links to approve/reject

## Message Format (from plan)
{
  "project_key": "<project_path>",
  "sender_name": "SLB-System",
  "to": ["SLB-Broadcast"],
  "subject": "[SLB] DANGEROUS: rm -rf ./build...",
  "body_md": "## Command Approval Request\n\n**ID**: req-abc\n**Risk**: dangerous\n**Command**: `rm -rf ./build`\n\n### Justification\n...\n\n---\nTo review: `slb review req-abc`\nTo approve: `slb approve req-abc --session-id <your-session>`",
  "importance": "normal",
  "thread_id": "SLB-Reviews"
}

## Importance Mapping
- CRITICAL → "urgent"
- DANGEROUS → "normal"
- CAUTION → "low"

## Functions (internal/integrations/agentmail.go)

### NewAgentMailClient(projectKey string) (*AgentMailClient, error)
- Initialize MCP client connection

### NotifyNewRequest(req *Request) error
- Format message
- Send via Agent Mail MCP

### NotifyRequestApproved(req *Request, review *Review) error
### NotifyRequestRejected(req *Request, review *Review) error
### NotifyRequestExecuted(req *Request, result *ExecutionResult) error

## Configuration
[integrations]
agent_mail_enabled = true
agent_mail_thread = "SLB-Reviews"

## Files
- internal/integrations/agentmail.go

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Notifications sent on request creation
- [ ] Thread grouping works
- [ ] Importance set correctly
- [ ] Redacted commands in messages
- [ ] Configurable enable/disable","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GreenCreek","created_at":"2025-12-13T18:26:33.636090057-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-13T20:50:38.191361284-06:00","closed_at":"2025-12-13T20:50:38.191367265-06:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"simultaneous_launch_button-d19.2","depends_on_id":"simultaneous_launch_button-3s8.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-13T18:26:33.636990294-06:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"simultaneous_launch_button-d19.3","title":"Implement Cursor rules generator","description":"## Task
Implement generator for Cursor IDE rules (.cursorrules).

## Command: slb integrations cursor-rules --install

## Generated Content
Appends to or creates .cursorrules file with:

```markdown
## Dangerous Command Policy (slb)

Before running any command that might be destructive, you MUST use slb for approval:

1. Check if command needs approval:
   \`slb patterns test "<command>"\`

2. If approval needed, request it:
   \`slb request "<command>" --reason "..." --expected-effect "..." --goal "..." --safety "..."\`

3. Wait for approval:
   \`slb status <request-id> --wait\`

4. Execute only when approved:
   \`slb execute <request-id>\`

### Risk Tiers
- 🔴 CRITICAL: Requires 2+ approvals (DROP DATABASE, terraform destroy)
- 🟠 DANGEROUS: Requires 1 approval (rm -rf, git reset --hard)
- 🟡 CAUTION: Auto-approved after 30s (rm *.log, git branch -d)

### Quick Reference
- Start session: \`slb session start --agent "<name>" --program "cursor" --model "<model>"\`
- Atomic run: \`slb run "<command>" --reason "..."\`
- Check pending: \`slb pending\`

Never bypass slb for dangerous commands. The point is peer review.
```

## Options
- --install: Write to .cursorrules
- --preview: Show what would be written
- --append: Append to existing (default)
- --replace: Replace existing slb section

## Files
- internal/integrations/cursor.go
- internal/cli/integrations.go (update)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Generates valid .cursorrules content
- [ ] Appends correctly to existing file
- [ ] Preview mode works
- [ ] Content includes all key instructions","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BrownCastle","created_at":"2025-12-13T18:33:39.692087451-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-13T20:19:56.583083564-06:00","closed_at":"2025-12-13T20:19:56.583083564-06:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"simultaneous_launch_button-d19.3","depends_on_id":"simultaneous_launch_button-qr8.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-13T18:33:39.69303687-06:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"simultaneous_launch_button-djg","title":"Unit Tests: DB Package (62% → 90% coverage)","description":"## Background and Purpose

The internal/db package implements all SQLite database operations for SLB and currently has 61.7% coverage - the highest in the codebase. This package needs to reach 90%+ coverage because it's the persistence layer where data integrity issues would be catastrophic.

## Current State

Existing test files (5):
- db_test.go: Schema, migrations, FTS triggers
- sessions_test.go: Session CRUD operations
- requests_test.go: Request CRUD operations
- reviews_test.go: Review CRUD operations
- outcomes_test.go: Execution outcome storage

Current coverage: 61.7%

## Coverage Gap Analysis

The 38.3% uncovered code likely includes:
- Complex query paths (search, filtering)
- Pagination edge cases
- Transaction rollback scenarios
- Constraint violation handling
- Concurrent write conflicts
- Migration upgrade paths

## Why 90% Coverage is Required

1. **Data Integrity**: All writes must be correct
2. **Query Correctness**: Wrong data returned = wrong decisions
3. **Transaction Safety**: Partial writes are unacceptable
4. **Migration Safety**: Schema changes must be tested

## Testing Strategy

### Coverage Improvement Plan

1. **Query Coverage Gaps**
   - Full-text search edge cases
   - Empty result handling
   - Large result sets
   - Filter combinations

2. **Transaction Gaps**
   - Rollback on error
   - Concurrent transactions
   - Deadlock handling
   - Connection pool exhaustion

3. **Constraint Gaps**
   - Unique constraint violations
   - Foreign key violations
   - Check constraint failures
   - Not null violations

4. **Migration Gaps**
   - Fresh database creation
   - Upgrade from each version
   - Downgrade handling (if supported)
   - Partial migration recovery

### New Test Files Needed
- db/search_test.go: Full-text search tests
- db/transaction_test.go: Transaction behavior
- db/migration_test.go: Schema migration tests
- db/concurrent_test.go: Concurrent access tests

## Dependencies
- DEPENDS ON: [Test Infrastructure bead] for DB helpers

## Success Criteria
- go test ./internal/db/... shows 90%+ coverage
- All constraint violations tested
- Transaction rollback verified
- FTS search comprehensive

## Implementation Notes

Test constraint violations:
  _, err := db.CreateSession(duplicateSession)
  require.ErrorIs(t, err, ErrActiveSessionExists)

Test FTS search:
  results, _ := db.SearchRequests('deploy AND production')
  require.Contains(t, results[0].Command, 'deploy')

Use -count=10 with -race to catch concurrency bugs.

## Estimated Effort: Medium","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BrownLake","created_at":"2025-12-13T21:01:20.971113396-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-13T22:48:04.702581686-06:00","closed_at":"2025-12-13T22:48:04.702581686-06:00","labels":["db","priority-high","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"simultaneous_launch_button-djg","depends_on_id":"simultaneous_launch_button-v2x","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-13T21:01:20.975023179-06:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"simultaneous_launch_button-ei2","title":"Implement TCP listener for Docker/remote agents","description":"## Task
Implement optional TCP listener for agents that can't access Unix socket.

## Background (from plan [daemon] section)
Docker agents can't access host Unix socket. TCP allows remote connection.

## Configuration
[daemon]
tcp_addr = "127.0.0.1:3333"     # Empty = disabled
tcp_require_auth = true          # Require session key in handshake
tcp_allowed_ips = ["127.0.0.1", "172.17.0.0/16"]  # Docker bridge

## Protocol
Same JSON-RPC as Unix socket, but with auth handshake:
1. Client connects
2. Client sends: {"auth": "session_key_here"}
3. Server validates, starts accepting requests

## SLB_HOST Environment Variable
Agents in Docker use: SLB_HOST=host.docker.internal:3333

## Files
- internal/daemon/ipc.go (extend)
- internal/daemon/tcp.go

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] TCP listener starts on configured port
- [ ] Auth handshake works
- [ ] IP whitelist enforced
- [ ] Falls back to Unix socket if TCP unavailable","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-13T18:48:50.373321179-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-13T21:52:42.122535473-06:00","closed_at":"2025-12-13T21:52:42.122535473-06:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"simultaneous_launch_button-ei2","depends_on_id":"simultaneous_launch_button-f4c.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-13T18:48:50.375493696-06:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"simultaneous_launch_button-eik","title":"Unit Tests: TUI Package (0% → 90% coverage)","description":"## Background and Purpose

The internal/tui package provides the terminal user interface for SLB using Bubble Tea and Lipgloss. Currently at 0% test coverage across all TUI subpackages (tui/*, theme, components), this package handles the interactive approval interface that operators use to review and approve requests.

## Current State

The TUI package includes:
- tui/tui.go: Main TUI model and initialization
- tui/views/: Different screen views (list, detail, approve)
- tui/components/: Reusable UI components (timeline, status)
- tui/theme/: Color themes and styling (Catppuccin)

Key TUI features:
- Request list with filtering
- Request detail view
- Approval/rejection workflow
- Timeline visualization
- Keyboard navigation
- Status indicators with colors

## Why TUI Testing is Critical

1. **Operator Interface**: Humans approve requests through TUI
2. **State Machine**: Complex view transitions must work
3. **Keyboard Handling**: All keybindings must respond correctly
4. **Visual Consistency**: Styling must apply consistently
5. **Accessibility**: Screen reader support future-proofing

## Testing Strategy

### Test Categories

1. **Model Tests**
   - Init() produces correct initial state
   - Update() handles all message types
   - View() returns renderable string
   - State transitions work correctly

2. **Component Tests**
   - Timeline renders events correctly
   - Status badges show correct colors
   - Progress bars calculate widths
   - Tables handle empty state

3. **Theme Tests**
   - All color constants defined
   - Styles compose correctly
   - Theme switching works

4. **Key Binding Tests**
   - All keys mapped correctly
   - Help text accurate
   - No conflicting bindings

### Test File Structure
- tui/tui_test.go: Main model tests
- tui/components/timeline_test.go: Timeline tests
- tui/theme/theme_test.go: Theme/style tests
- tui/views/list_test.go: List view tests

## Dependencies
- DEPENDS ON: [Test Infrastructure bead] for model test helpers

## Success Criteria
- go test ./internal/tui/... shows 90%+ coverage
- All state transitions tested
- Key bindings verified
- Theme colors accessible

## Implementation Notes

Use Bubble Tea's testing patterns:
  m := NewModel()
  m, cmd := m.Update(tea.KeyMsg{Type: tea.KeyEnter})
  // Assert on model state

For view tests, use string assertions on rendered output.
Tea.Msg types to test: KeyMsg, WindowSizeMsg, custom messages.

Note: Visual testing is limited - focus on state logic, not pixel-perfect rendering.

## Estimated Effort: Medium","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-13T21:00:02.95021837-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-13T23:14:33.511550053-06:00","closed_at":"2025-12-13T23:14:33.511550053-06:00","labels":["priority-medium","testing","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"simultaneous_launch_button-eik","depends_on_id":"simultaneous_launch_button-v2x","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-13T21:00:02.953704194-06:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"simultaneous_launch_button-f0i","title":"Phase 2: Business Logic Extraction for Testability","description":"## Purpose & Rationale

This phase addresses the hardest testability challenge: 11 functions at 0% coverage that contain embedded business logic mixed with system calls. The key insight is that some of these functions contain valuable, testable business logic that's currently untestable because it's coupled to:
- os.Exit() calls
- exec.Command execution
- Long-running polling loops
- Signal handling

By extracting the pure business logic into separate, testable functions, we can:
1. Test the decision-making code directly
2. Leave the "glue code" with system calls at 0% (acceptable)
3. Significantly raise coverage with minimal code changes

## Functions Analysis

### High-Value Extraction Candidates

**pollRequests (watch.go:151)**
- Current: 0% coverage
- Contains: Request filtering logic, state machine for approval workflow
- Extract: `evaluatePendingRequest(req *Request, session *Session, config *Config) (action, error)`
- This pure function can be thoroughly unit tested

**autoApproveCaution (watch.go:223)**
- Current: 0% coverage  
- Contains: Auto-approval safety decision logic
- Extract: `shouldAutoApprove(req *Request, config *AutoApproveConfig) (bool, reason string)`
- Critical safety code that MUST be tested

**runSafeCommand (run.go:181)**
- Current: 0% coverage
- Contains: Command classification, risk tier determination
- Extract: `classifyCommandRisk(cmd string, patterns []Pattern) RiskTier`
- Already partially exists in patterns package, but CLI layer has additional logic

### Low-Value (Acceptable at 0%)

**Execute (root.go:106)**
- Simple main entry point, just calls rootCmd.Execute()
- Testing requires os.Exit capture, not worth the complexity
- Accept 0% - covered by integration tests

**writeError (run.go:356)**
- Simple file write wrapper
- Low complexity, tested implicitly
- Accept 0% or trivial test

**createRunLogFile (run.go:297)**
- File creation with timestamp
- Could add simple mock test if needed
- Low priority

## Extraction Pattern

```go
// BEFORE: Untestable
func pollRequests(ctx context.Context, db *DB, config *Config) error {
    reqs, _ := db.ListPending()
    for _, req := range reqs {
        // Complex decision logic mixed with DB calls
        if req.ShouldAutoApprove(config) { ... }
    }
}

// AFTER: Testable core + thin wrapper
func evaluatePendingRequest(req *Request, config *Config) Decision {
    // Pure business logic, no side effects
}

func pollRequests(ctx context.Context, db *DB, config *Config) error {
    reqs, _ := db.ListPending()
    for _, req := range reqs {
        decision := evaluatePendingRequest(req, config) // testable!
        applyDecision(db, req, decision) // glue code
    }
}
```

## Success Criteria
- At least 3 core logic functions extracted and tested
- Each extraction has comprehensive unit tests
- Original function signatures unchanged (no breaking changes)
- Coverage increases by ~10-15%

## Risk Assessment
- LOW: Pure function extraction is a safe refactoring pattern
- Validate: Run all existing tests after each extraction
- Rollback: Each extraction is independent and revertible","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T10:26:09.628739873-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T14:01:36.654954856-06:00","closed_at":"2025-12-16T14:01:36.654954856-06:00","close_reason":"Phase 2 Complete: All three extraction tasks done. P2.1: evaluateRequestForPolling+statusToEventType (100%), P2.2: shouldAutoApproveCaution (100%), P2.3: runSafeCommand analyzed as glue code - acceptable 0%. Coverage improved from 57.7% to 59.8%.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"simultaneous_launch_button-f0i","depends_on_id":"simultaneous_launch_button-9c0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T10:27:29.434431135-06:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
